data.title: Planet Evening News
data.description: 1. Data: Kalshi's notional trading volume in March is expected to reach $12.6 billion; 2. US Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $93.1 million this week; 3. Robinhood CEO urges Congress to allow stablecoin yields; 4. Strategy CEO: Morgan Stanley's allocation of 2% to Bitcoin would bring in approximately $160 billion in funds; 5. The number of addresses holding less than 100 XRP on the XRP Ledger reached a record high of 5.66 million; 6. Citibank: Bitcoin may reach $165,000 this year; 7. CryptoQuant analyst: Coinbase premium has recovered to neutral, but the bullish momentum of US funds remains unconfirmed; 8. Whale "tummy.hl" opened a 3x leveraged long position in HYPE, currently holding a position worth approximately $5.33 million; 9. A trader created a new wallet "mzandres" and invested $26,000 to predict that US troops would enter Iran before the end of March.

data.title: Severe Flooding in Hawaii Prompts Evacuations
data.description:
Heavy rainfall in Hawaii has led to flooding, prompting evacuation orders for approximately 5,500 residents north of Honolulu. According to RTHK, local officials on Friday morning urged residents to evacuate areas downstream of the Wahiawa Dam, which is at imminent risk of breaching. A city spokesperson reported no casualties so far, and rescue teams have been dispatched to assist those trapped by the floodwaters.

Honolulu Mayor Rick Blangiardi stated that the heavy rains have damaged dozens, if not hundreds, of homes, although a comprehensive assessment of the damage is not yet possible. Hawaii Governor Josh Green announced on social media that the National Guard has been deployed to respond to the disaster.

data.title: Egypt to Settle $1.3 Billion Debt to International Oil Companies by End of June
data.description: Egypt's Ministry of Petroleum has announced plans to pay off $1.3 billion in outstanding debts to international oil companies by the end of June. According to Jin10, this move is part of Egypt's efforts to improve its financial standing and foster better relations with foreign investors in the energy sector. The payment is expected to enhance Egypt's credibility and attract further investment in its oil and gas industry. The Ministry emphasized the importance of timely payments to maintain trust and encourage continued collaboration with international partners.

data.title: Elon Musk Offers to Pay TSA Salaries Amid U.S. Government Funding Stalemate
data.description: Elon Musk has proposed to cover the salaries of Transportation Security Administration (TSA) personnel during the ongoing U.S. government funding impasse. According to Jin10, this offer comes as the government faces a budgetary deadlock, affecting various federal operations. Musk's gesture aims to alleviate the financial strain on TSA employees who are impacted by the funding stalemate. The situation highlights the broader challenges faced by federal workers amid political gridlock in Washington.

data.title: Iranian Navy guides Indian oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz
data.description: According to a senior officer on board, the Iranian Navy recently guided an Indian liquefied petroleum gas tanker through the Strait of Hormuz. Following diplomatic intervention from New Delhi, the ship was able to pass along a pre-approved route. This account appears to confirm analysts' views that Tehran is attempting to implement traffic control in the strait, allowing friendly vessels safe passage while raising concerns about attack on other ships. The officer stated that during the transit, his vessel maintained radio contact with the Iranian Navy. The Iranian side recorded the ship's flag, name, port of origin, port of destination, and the nationalities of the crew (all Indian), and guided them along the pre-arranged route. (Jin Shi)

data.title: India's Prime Minister Modi Emphasizes Importance of Navigational Freedom
data.description: India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi has reiterated the significance of maintaining freedom of navigation and ensuring the safety and openness of maritime routes. According to Jin10, Modi's statement underscores India's commitment to upholding international maritime laws and promoting regional stability. The emphasis on navigational freedom aligns with India's strategic interests in fostering secure and open sea lanes, which are vital for global trade and economic growth. Modi's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about maritime security and the need for cooperative efforts to address challenges in the region.

data.title: India's Prime Minister Modi Praises Iran for Ensuring Safety of Indian Nationals
data.description: India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi has expressed appreciation for Iran's ongoing support in ensuring the safety and security of Indian nationals residing in Iran. According to Jin10, Modi's remarks highlight the cooperative relationship between the two countries in safeguarding their citizens abroad. This acknowledgment comes amid broader discussions on regional security and diplomatic ties between India and Iran. The Indian government continues to prioritize the welfare of its citizens overseas, emphasizing the importance of international collaboration in achieving this goal.

data.title: India's Prime Minister Modi Condemns Attacks on Key Regional Infrastructure
data.description: India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi has condemned recent attacks on critical infrastructure in the region, emphasizing that such actions pose a threat to regional stability and disrupt global supply chains. According to Jin10, Modi's statement highlights the broader implications of these attacks, which not only affect local stability but also have far-reaching consequences for international trade and economic networks. The Prime Minister's remarks underscore the importance of safeguarding infrastructure to maintain both regional and global economic stability.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports: U.S.-Israeli Coalition Attacks Naval Docks in Southern Iran
data.description: Early this morning, a joint U.S.-Israeli military operation targeted two naval docks in Bushehr, southern Iran. According to Jin10, the attack resulted in damage to maritime facilities and docking points, with several ships destroyed. The incident underscores ongoing tensions in the region, as geopolitical dynamics continue to evolve. Iranian media, Tasnim News Agency, reported the details of the attack, highlighting the strategic significance of the location and the potential implications for regional security.

data.title: India's Prime Minister Modi Discusses with Iran's President Pezehchiyan
data.description: India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi recently held a conversation with Iran's President Ebrahim Pezehchiyan. According to Jin10, the discussion between the two leaders focused on strengthening bilateral relations and exploring areas of mutual interest. The dialogue underscores the ongoing diplomatic efforts between India and Iran to enhance cooperation in various sectors. Both leaders emphasized the importance of maintaining strong ties and working together on regional and international issues.

data.title: Rival Companies Form Unlikely Alliance Amid Market Challenges
data.description: In a surprising turn of events, former competitors have joined forces to navigate the current market landscape. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the unusual collaboration driven by a mix of economic pressures and strategic interests. This alliance marks a significant shift in the industry, as companies seek to leverage each other's strengths to overcome shared challenges. The partnership underscores the evolving dynamics within the market, where adaptability and cooperation are becoming increasingly vital for success.

data.title: BTS Discusses New Album and Cultural Identity in Exclusive Interview
data.description: In a recent interview, BTS shared insights into their latest album, Arirang, emphasizing the cultural significance and personal connections that influenced its creation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the group's desire to present a unified message to the international community. The members expressed the importance of their cultural roots in shaping their identity and music, aiming to connect deeply with audiences worldwide. The album reflects their journey and aspirations, showcasing a blend of traditional and contemporary elements that resonate with their global fanbase.

data.title: Russia Proposes Supplying Power Equipment to India, Indian Official Says
data.description: Russia has proposed supplying power equipment to India, according to an Indian power official. This development highlights the ongoing collaboration between the two countries in the energy sector. According to Jin10, the proposal aims to enhance India's power infrastructure and support its growing energy needs. The offer from Russia comes as India continues to expand its energy capacity to meet increasing domestic demand. Further details on the proposal and potential agreements between the two nations have yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Citibank: Bitcoin may reach $165,000 this year.
data.description: A report released by Citibank indicates that Bitcoin could reach $165,000 this year. The bank manages $2.5 trillion in assets. (The Bitcoin Historian)

data.title: Israel Accuses Iran of Attacking Kindergarten
data.description: Israel's Foreign Ministry spokesperson has accused the Iranian regime of launching an attack on an Israeli kindergarten. According to Jin10, the spokesperson stated that the attack specifically targeted children, escalating tensions between the two nations. This incident adds to the ongoing hostilities in the region, with both countries frequently exchanging accusations and threats. The Israeli government has condemned the attack and is expected to take further diplomatic and security measures in response. The situation remains tense as international observers call for restraint and dialogue to prevent further escalation.

data.title: GMX: Publicly recruiting a CEO to lead its next phase of expansion.
data.description: GMX announced on its X platform that it is hiring a CEO, marking a milestone in the project's governance. The role will involve leading strategy, formalizing the organization, strengthening partnerships, and guiding GMX into its next phase of expansion. GMX is seeking a leader with expertise in decentralized infrastructure and how it scales, and applications are open to qualified candidates from any background.

data.title: UAE Defense Ministry: Interception of Missiles and Drones from Iran
data.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) Defense Ministry announced the interception of three ballistic missiles and eight drones on March 21, which were launched from Iran. According to Jin10, since the onset of attacks from Iran, the UAE's air defense systems have successfully intercepted a total of 341 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, and 1,748 drones.

data.title: SIREN Tokens Transferred from DWF Labs to Anonymous Address
data.description: At 18:09, 2,999,900 SIREN tokens were transferred from DWF Labs to an anonymous address starting with 0x4Df6. According to ChainCatcher, the value of these tokens is approximately $2.73 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Market Experiences Unusual Whale Activity Amid Price Fluctuations
data.description:
Bitcoin has witnessed significant whale activity, according to PANews. On March 11 and 13, as Bitcoin rebounded to $70,000-$71,000, whale addresses holding over 1,000 BTC each sold 24,867 and 17,818 BTC, respectively. The March 11 sale resulted in an average loss of 50% compared to the cost, while the March 13 sale incurred a loss of less than 10%.

Analysis indicates these sales originated from purchases made at low points in May-July and early April 2025. Some investors exited with substantial losses, while others rotated out after precise bottom-buying. On March 17, Strategy acquired 22,337 BTC, absorbing much of the excess supply.

Despite these movements, the market sentiment remains fragile, with investors holding BTC for nearly a year showing signs of dwindling patience. Future price rebounds may still trigger pressure from loss-incurring holdings.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Decline Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
Bitcoin has experienced a nearly 5% decline this week, influenced by the ongoing conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran. According to Odaily, this downturn aligns with simultaneous drops in the S&P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq, and gold, while crude oil has surged by 7.3%, marking a 53% increase since the conflict began on February 28.

The Kobeissi Letter reports that over the past three months, there has been a record outflow of $64 billion from the S&P 500 ETF and Nasdaq 100 ETF, accounting for about 5% of total assets under management. Additionally, spot Bitcoin ETFs have seen a net outflow of $253 million in the last two days.

Data from Glassnode indicates that the market is struggling to absorb selling pressure, with Bitcoin's net realized profit-taking accelerating to approximately $17 million per hour at one point, before losing momentum and falling below $70,000. Analysts suggest that geopolitical uncertainties have compressed market demand depth, making it challenging to absorb even moderate sell-offs.

Historical patterns suggest that Bitcoin's current trajectory mirrors its behavior during the Russia-Ukraine conflict, where initial sell-offs were followed by short-term rebounds, only to face continued downward pressure. Analysts believe that rising energy costs, liquidity constraints, and forced selling are prolonging Bitcoin's recovery. Finish anticipates that Bitcoin may find a bottom around $55,000 before gradually recovering, but market caution is expected to persist until the Iran conflict is resolved.
